# AI Agent Guardrails

> Open-source safety patterns for AI agents that handle real money.

## Core Principle
LLMs understand language. Code enforces rules. Never confuse the two. Your LLM should figure out WHAT the user wants. Your code should decide WHETHER it's allowed and WHETHER it's safe.

## Three-Layer Architecture
Layer 1 (LLM): Intent detection, classification, context resolution.
Layer 2 (Code): Operation completeness validation, identity verification, rate limiting, business rules.
Layer 3 (Code): Deduplication, platform restrictions, transaction simulation.

## Key Patterns

### Two-Call LLM Architecture
Call 1: Classify intent (operational/educational/conversational). Call 2: Extract structured parameters. Deterministic code validates between and after both calls.

### Operation Completeness
Define required fields per operation type in code: buy needs (amount, token_out, chain), sell needs (amount, token, chain), send needs (amount, token, recipient, chain), bridge needs (amount, token, chain, target_chain).

### Identity Verification
Check operation.user_id === authenticatedUserId in code. Never let the LLM determine the user. Pass auth from session/JWT directly to validation.

### Rate Limiting
Tiered: global (ops/minute, ops/hour), per-type (sends/hour), value-based (max USD per transaction, daily volume). All in code.

### Hallucination Prevention
Whitelist valid operation types. Validate every LLM output against the list. Remove hallucinated types. When uncertain, return LESS not MORE.

### Prompt Hardening
Security rules in LLM prompts as defense-in-depth. Anti-impersonation, anti-manipulation, safe defaults. Not sufficient alone, code validates everything.

## File Structure
- examples/templates/ — LLM prompt templates (Call 1 and Call 2)
- examples/validation/ — Deterministic validation (completeness, hallucination checks)
- examples/rate-limiting/ — Rate limiter implementation
- examples/security/ — Identity verification, deduplication, prompt hardening, platform restrictions
- examples/middleware/ — Full pipeline example showing all layers
- docs/ — Architecture deep dive, threat model, and LLM perspective on building safely
- src/core/types.ts — Shared type definitions

## Built By
Quick Intel and Tator team, production-tested across 60+ blockchain networks.
Repository: https://github.com/azep-ninja/ai-agent-guardrails
